mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed negative returns in more than 65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack signals impending economic deceleration, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and declining interest rates creates a favorable earnings growth environment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns as corporate revenues expand while financing costs contract.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in multivariate regression, or the combined signal yields negative returns in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term equity momentum through improved corporate output and inventory turnover over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during supply-constrained regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO momentum, yielding a Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict return direction with an out-of-sample accuracy below 52%, or the signal decays to zero predictive power after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 260, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL breaches 260, or the threshold effect disappears when adjusting for nominal GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in unemployment and rise in housing starts signals synchronized labor and housing market strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns through broad-based economic expansion and financial sector leverage.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in panel regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real GDP growth below trend during a late-cycle phase triggers recession pricing, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors discount future earnings contractions over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 deceleration fails to predict negative returns with a Sharpe ratio below -0.5 in forward return portfolios, or the signal performs identically across all business cycle phases.
